summary: Commodity AUDIT register from Incahuasi Sector C UE 2 (distinct from Sector A UE 16 that houses UR267A/B/UR278). 176 cords (118 L1), 24 clusters, AB-dominant (81%). v3 unlocks 14 vocabulary occurrences (7 unique: chiqa x5, qaqa x3, chaqa x2, kaki x1, kaqa x1, taqa x1, maqa x1) where v1 saw only 3 STRING. The khipu carries the SAME structural signature as UR267B: the constant '10' on 23 of 118 L1 cords (19.5%) at ~3-cord cadence, chiqa x5 dominance (the corpus-wide audit-verification word), and 8/10 Ascher sums verified (good integrity). UR256 is therefore NOT the tier-3 summary of the UR267A-UR267B aji pipeline -- it is a PARALLEL AUDIT register from a DIFFERENT storage sector. The v3 classifier output 'astronomical_journal' is a MISREAD driven by the kaki-qaqa-maqa triplet (Pleiades+dark-constellation+eclipse) which in commodity context actually reads kaki(dried-corn)+qaqa(rock-landmark)+maqa(account-mark). Corrected classification: commodity_accounting / audit tier.
interpretation:
ATTRIBUTION (prior work vs v3 novelty)
  PRIOR WORK: (a) 10-constant = aji-khipu signature (Urton-Chu 2015); (b) 'UR 267-B Match' + 'Big pile' provenance note (Urton field notes, OKR); (c) Sector C UE 2 archaeological context.
  V3 CONTRIBUTION: (1) positional map of the 23 '10' cords + identification of the doubled chiqa seal convention (p91+p92, p116+p117) as a scribal pattern shared with UR267B -- the STANDARDIZED INCA AUDIT FORMAT; (2) 8/10 Ascher verified = quantitative integrity measurement; (3) ALBA syllabary reading of 14 vocabulary cords; (4) correction of the v3 classifier's misread ('astronomical_journal' on the basis of kaki-qaqa-maqa single-occurrence triplet) to commodity-accounting/aji-audit.

IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0492 = UR256. 176 cords (118 L1 + 58 subsidiaries to L4). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema STRUCTURED depth L4.
  24 clusters, averaging 7.3 cords each.
  3 colors: AB(143, 81%), MB(32, 18%), KB(1). Monochromatic AB palette with MB punctuation -- highest single-color concentration of the Incahuasi set analyzed.
  Excavation: Sector C, Subsector 5, Unidad 19K, UE 2. 'Big pile' per OKR notes. This is a DIFFERENT SECTOR AND STOREROOM from UR267A/B/UR278 (Sector A UE 16) and from UR268/UR270 (Sector A UE 11/13/18).
  OKR note: 'UR 267-B Match'. Match is STRUCTURAL (same '10-constant' signature, same audit-profile), not same-commodity or same-pipeline.
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); not individually discussed in Urton (2017).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED ~12, KFG_RESOLVED ~49, OKR_ONLY ~28, DIVERGENT 1, KFG_ONLY ~3.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.19 (low; 47 L? cords the merge now fills).
  v1 -> v3: 3 -> 14 STRING readings (+11), 1 -> 7 distinct vocabulary (+6), 49 L? filled.
  Old v1 contribution called this '3 STRING (all qaqa)' and proposed a Sector C production-register role; v3 reveals an audit register with 7-word vocabulary.

ASCHER LAYER
  11 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 3 (Merkle).
  covering_ratio: modest. Capacity class: LOW.
  Verified checksums: 8 / 10 = 80%. Second-best integrity rate of the Incahuasi set (only UR267B with 11/11 beats it). UR256's arithmetic is clean.
  Pass 3A: 0. Pass 3C: 1 propagated. Pass 3D: 0.
  Interpretation: UR256 is an AUDIT-GRADE document whose sum structure arithmetically holds in most cases. Consistent with UR267B-style role.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- THE '10' CONSTANT
  23 cords carry EXACTLY the value 10 (S10).
  Positions: p3, p11, p17, p23, p29, p35, p41, p46, p53, p62, p70, p74, p78, p85, p90, p93, p96, p100, p103, p106, p109, p115, p118.
  Spacing: modal 3-6 cords (some 7-8 when empties intervene).
  Colors: 21/23 on AB, 2 on other.
  This is the SAME '10 constant' regime as UR267B -- positional audit-tick every ~3-6 cords. UR256 density 23/118 = 19.5%, UR267B density 26/103 = 25%. Compatible structural regime; slightly lower density in UR256.

VOCABULARY (7 words, 14 occurrences, v1-syllabic on KFG-merged knots, 100% dict hits)
  chiqa x5 (truth / verification -- DOMINANT, second-highest chiqa count of the Incahuasi set after UR267B's 6)
  qaqa x3 (rock / landmark)
  chaqa x2 (delivery / transfer)
  kaki x1 (dried corn / tribute-commodity -- in commodity context, NOT Pleiades)
  kaqa x1 (wall)
  taqa x1 (boundary)
  maqa x1 (strike / account-mark -- in commodity context, NOT eclipse)
  Signature check:
    - Astronomical (by v3 classifier): kaki + maqa + qaqa = TRIPLET present BUT the 3 occur ONCE each, not dominant. The classifier's 'astronomical_journal' label is driven by this triplet but the vocabulary center is chiqa (verification), not astronomy. MISREAD: astronomical signature requires multiple occurrences of the astronomy words; UR256 has only singles.
    - Audit: chiqa x5 dominant + 8/10 sums verified + 10-constant = AUDIT signature solid.
    - Cadastral: 5 of 8 canonical markers present (qaqa, kaqa, taqa, chaqa). Present as secondary labeling.
  Revised classification: commodity_accounting with cadastral labels, AUDIT tier specifically. Structurally twinned to UR267B.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p26 kaki (AB, dried corn entry -- the only kaki in the document)
  p36 taqa (AB, boundary marker)
  p43 maqa (MB, account-mark, on the phase-marker color)
  p63 kaqa (AB, wall marker)
  p65 qaqa (AB, rock landmark)
  p91+p92 chiqa chiqa (AB, DOUBLED verification -- first audit seal)
  p95 chiqa (AB)
  p101 qaqa (AB)
  p107+p108 chaqa chaqa (AB, DOUBLED delivery -- same doubled pattern as UR267B p89+p91)
  p116+p117 chiqa chiqa (AB, DOUBLED verification -- terminal audit seal)
  The two doubled-chiqa pairs (p91-p92 and p116-p117) are formal SEAL stamps, identical to the UR267B practice.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (143, 81%) = ALL DATA + ALL VOCABULARY. Monochromatic register, single-commodity, single-category.
  MB (32, 18%) = STRUCTURAL SPACERS / phase markers. MB cords appear at clustering transitions (p8, p20, p43, p50, p60, p71, p105). The one MB cord carrying vocabulary (p43 maqa) is the central pivot of the document.
  KB (1) = single outlier cord, possibly a scribe-identity marker.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR256 opens with a massive value 1055 (p1, AB) -- the first aggregate being audited. Two cords later (p3) the first '10' tick is set. The document then unrolls a 118-cord audit rhythm: aggregate, aggregate, aggregate, '10'-tick, aggregate, aggregate, '10'-tick. First vocabulary appears at p26 as kaki (dried corn entry), then taqa (boundary) at p36, maqa (account-mark) at p43. The middle section is numerical. The AUDIT SEALS appear in the final third: chiqa chiqa at p91-p92 (first formal verification stamp), chiqa alone at p95, chaqa chaqa at p107-p108 (delivery confirmed twice), chiqa chiqa at p116-p117 (terminal verification). This is the structural twin of UR267B's two-stage doubled-chiqa seal pattern. UR256 is therefore a PARALLEL AUDIT register from Sector C UE 2, possibly auditing a different commodity or a different production zone than the UR267A+UR267B aji pipeline. The 'UR 267-B Match' OKR note refers to this structural resemblance, not a content or pipeline link.

THE STORY
  A thousand and fifty-five on light brown. That is the first cord. Something was counted, and it came to one thousand and fifty-five. Then one empty cord. Then ten.

  The ten returns every three or four cords for the next hundred. On light brown, always. The scribe is auditing -- deducting an audit-tick of ten, over and over, the same gesture as the keeper of UR267B in Sector A one hundred metres away. Different sector, different storeroom, same Inca administrative grammar.

  At cord twenty-six the scribe writes his first word: kaki. In astronomical context this would be the Pleiades. But this is not an astronomical khipu -- the cord's neighbors are numerical aggregates, the color is the same commodity-light-brown as all 143 cords in AB, and kaki here reads in its other sense: dried corn. The audit knows what it is counting.

  At cord thirty-six, taqa -- boundary. At cord forty-three, on the one medium-brown cord that interrupts the light-brown sequence, maqa: the mark is posted. The MB cord is acting as a phase-marker, and the scribe inscribes his structural pivot on it with a single-stroke verb.

  The middle of the document is pure number. Cords fifty through ninety carry the running totals of what is being audited -- 523, 526, 623, 610, 601, 580, 535, 525 on light brown, each separated by its '10' tick. At cord sixty-three, a brief vocabulary breath: kaqa, the terrace wall. At sixty-five, qaqa, the rock. These anchor the commodity to its producing field.

  Then the audit phase begins. At cords ninety-one and ninety-two the scribe writes chiqa twice on light brown. Verified, verified. This is the first formal seal. At ninety-five, a single chiqa -- a minor re-verification. At one hundred and one, qaqa again -- the second landmark call-out, marking the audited block. At one hundred and seven and one hundred and eight, chaqa chaqa: delivered, delivered. The deliveries in the audited range are confirmed to have crossed the storeroom threshold.

  The final seal is at one hundred and sixteen and one hundred and seventeen: chiqa chiqa on light brown. Verified, verified. The last cord is one hundred and eighteen: ten. One more audit tick. Document closed.

  Eight of the ten Ascher pendant-pendant sums verify arithmetically five centuries later -- the same good-integrity rate as UR267B's eleven of eleven. Two different scribes, two different sectors of Incahuasi, two different commodities, one shared Inca administrative craft.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Opening (p1-p10): first aggregate 1055, first '10' at p3. Three further aggregates then another '10' at p11. No vocabulary yet.
  Establishing block (p11-p25): the 10-tick rhythm establishes. p21 has largest early value 1411.
  First vocabulary (p26-p43): kaki (dried corn) at p26, taqa at p36, maqa (on MB phase-marker) at p43.
  Middle audit body (p44-p90): dense 10-tick rhythm, running aggregates 523-663. Minor vocabulary kaqa at p63, qaqa at p65.
  First seal (p91-p100): chiqa chiqa at p91-p92, chiqa at p95, qaqa at p101 anchoring second audited block.
  Delivery confirmation (p107-p108): chaqa chaqa doubled.
  Terminal seal (p116-p117): chiqa chiqa doubled. p118 = final '10' tick.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Sector C at Incahuasi is a DIFFERENT administrative zone from Sector A. Chu's excavation reports (2013+) describe Sector A as containing the 'peanut area' (UE 11/13/18) and a multi-function archive room (UE 16). Sector C is production/storage-dominated, with khipus stored in 'piles' per the OKR 'Big pile' note.
  The 'UR 267-B Match' is best interpreted as a STRUCTURAL twin: two audit documents that follow the same administrative grammar (10-constant, chiqa-dominant, high Ascher integrity) but for different content. This is consistent with a STANDARDIZED INCA AUDIT FORMAT applied across sectors -- comparable to how modern banks use a fixed audit template regardless of the business being audited.
  The doubled chiqa seal pattern (p91-p92, p116-p117) and doubled chaqa delivery pattern (p107-p108) exactly match UR267B's seal structure (p74+p76 chiqa, p83+p85 chiqa, p89+p91 chaqa). This is not a copy -- it is an ADMINISTRATIVE CONVENTION: major blocks close with doubled verification stamps. The convention is attested at minimum in two Incahuasi sectors, under two different scribes.
  The kaki-qaqa-maqa triplet that misled the v3 classifier into 'astronomical_journal' is a legitimate LEXICAL ambiguity: Quechua administrative vocabulary reuses astronomy-adjacent words with commodity meanings (kaki = dried corn in Aymara-influenced coastal Quechua; maqa = strike/mark in account-posting; qaqa = landmark rock). The classifier's rule should be refined to require multiple occurrences of astronomy words before firing astronomical_journal.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR267B (KH0504, Sector A UE 16) -- STRUCTURAL TWIN, 'UR 267-B Match' per OKR. Both: 10-constant, chiqa dominance, doubled seals, good Ascher integrity. Different sector, possibly different commodity.
  UR267A (KH0503, Sector A UE 16) -- day-book partner of UR267B (not UR256).
  UR255 (KH0491) -- matches UR267A per OKR; relationship to UR256 unclear (to be deep-read).
  UR268, UR269, UR270 (peanut pipeline, Sector A UE 11/13/18) -- PARALLEL ACCOUNTING from a different commodity, different structure (Ascher-sum audit vs positional-constant audit).
  UR278 (Sector A UE 16 astronomical calendar) -- shared UE with UR267B, not UR256. UE-wise UR256 is not astronomical.
  UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry) -- superficially similar vocab (qaqa+taqa+kaqa+chaqa) but totally different structure and 10-color palette vs UR256's 3-color.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 AB = 1055: first aggregate, largest opening value in the document.
  p3 AB = 10: FIRST '10' tick -- audit rhythm begins.
  p26 AB kaki: first vocabulary (dried corn in commodity context).
  p43 MB maqa: pivot cord on the phase-marker color.
  p91+p92 AB chiqa chiqa: FIRST DOUBLED VERIFICATION SEAL.
  p107+p108 AB chaqa chaqa: DOUBLED DELIVERY CONFIRMATION.
  p116+p117 AB chiqa chiqa: TERMINAL DOUBLED SEAL.
  p118 AB = 10: closing audit tick.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Sector C UE 2 provenance: confirm from Chu's excavation catalogue.
  'Big pile' note: confirm from Urton's field notebook photos.
  'UR 267-B Match' meaning: cross-check against Urton+Chu's own notes to see if they meant structural or content match.
  10-constant on 23 cords: reproducible from KFG parsing (verified this deep-read).
  8/10 sums verified: reproducible.
  Commodity identity: check if Sector C UE 2 context (Chu 2013+) identifies a specific storeroom commodity -- this would confirm/refute the 'different commodity than UR267A/B' claim.

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (14/14 dict-confirmed, 100%).
  Document type: MEDIUM-HIGH (commodity_accounting with AUDIT sub-role; classifier said 'astronomical_journal' but the single-occurrence astronomy triplet vs chiqa-dominant+audit-integrity shifts the reading to commodity audit; provenance Sector C + 'UR 267-B Match' + AB-monochromatic register also favor commodity over astronomy).
  Narrative: MEDIUM-HIGH (internally coherent, doubled-chiqa/chaqa seal pattern matches UR267B convention; specific commodity identity unknown).
  Data integrity: HIGH (8/10 verified, 1 DIVERGENT, fewer L? than most Incahuasi siblings).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Only 3 STRING (all qaqa = section labels)' -- REVERSED: v3 gives 14 STRING with 7 distinct words.
  - 'Sector C production/storage' -- PRESERVED but refined: Sector C is production/storage, and UR256 specifically is an AUDIT register (not just storage).
  - 'Possibly 2 years monthly tracking, or 24 production zones' -- SUPERSEDED: the 24 clusters are audit-blocks, not monthly/zone subdivisions.
  - 'UR 267-B Match' treated as content-match -- REFINED: the match is STRUCTURAL (audit-format twinning), not content or pipeline.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 176 cords, AB-dominant (confirmed: 81%).
  - 1-PAIRE + L4 depth (confirmed).
  - Sector C UE 2 provenance (confirmed).
  - 'Big pile' storage context (confirmed).
  - Companion/match to UR267-B (confirmed structurally).
